84.vague_question: If a candidate just wins a seat, how does that change their chance of both running again and winning the next election compared to just losing?

85.vague_question: For candidates who barely won or lost, how much did winning change the later value of government contracts going to the companies that funded them? We estimate the average change in contract value.

86.causal_question: What is the average causal effect, at the alignment threshold, of an MLA being from the MP's party on per-project MPLADS expenditure for projects proposed within two years after a state election?
86.vague_question: How much more money does an MP spend on a local project when the area's state legislator is from the same party, for projects proposed soon after state elections? This focuses on places right at the margin between aligned and not aligned.

87.causal_question: Among voters in U.S. House districts decided at an infinitesimally small margin, what is the average effect of being represented by a copartisan, compared to an outpartisan, on the probability of reporting contact with the representative?
87.vague_question: When your party barely wins versus barely loses your House district, how much does that change the chance you say you contacted your representative?

88.causal_question: Among districts where the previous U.S. House race was decided by an infinitesimal margin, what is the average effect of the Democratic party holding that House seat on the subsequent Democratic presidential vote share in that district during November elections?
88.vague_question: In very close House races, how much does having the same party hold the House seat raise that party's presidential vote share in the district? Focused on November elections.

89.causal_question: Among states with gubernatorial elections decided near 50-50, what is the average effect of electing a Democratic rather than a Republican governor on the one-year change in state policy liberalism?
89.vague_question: In a very close governor's race, if a Democrat wins instead of a Republican, how much does the state's overall policy orientation shift in the following year?

90.causal_question: Among individuals whose schooling increased due to compulsory schooling laws and who were born near the reform thresholds, what is the effect of one additional year of completed secondary schooling on the probability of saying at least one immigrant group should be allowed 'none' to enter?
90.vague_question: For people whose schooling increased because of a law change, how did one extra year of school affect whether they said some types of immigrants should not be allowed in?

91.causal_question: Among individuals whose schooling increases due to higher compulsory schooling requirements in these countries (compliers near eligibility), what is the change in the probability of endorsing restrictive immigration from one additional year of secondary schooling?
91.vague_question: For students kept in school one extra year because the leaving age was raised, how much does this reduce the chance they support restricting immigration? This is averaged across the countries studied.

92.causal_question: What is the average causal effect of casting a ballot in 2008 on the probability of voting in 2010 for individuals at the margin of voting in 2008?
92.vague_question: If someone votes in 2008 when they are on the cusp of voting, how much more likely are they to vote again in 2010? This focuses on people for whom voting in 2008 was a close call.

93.causal_question: Among parents whose child's age is arbitrarily close to the voting-age threshold, what is the average effect of the child being just eligible to vote on the parent's probability of voting in 2009?
93.vague_question: How much more likely do parents vote when their child has just become old enough to vote, compared with parents whose child is just not old enough, in 2009?

94.causal_question: Among Danish parents whose child's age is near the voting eligibility threshold in 2013, what is the average causal effect of the child becoming eligible to vote on the parent's probability of voting in that election?
94.vague_question: When a child just becomes old enough to vote, how much does that change their parent's chance of voting in 2013? Focus on parents with children close to the age cutoff.

95.causal_question: What is the average effect on a parent's probability of voting when their child becomes newly eligible to vote in the 2014 election?
95.vague_question: When a child first becomes old enough to vote, does that make their parent more likely to vote in 2014? If so, by how much?

96.causal_question: Among Danish parents whose child's age lies just around the voting-eligibility threshold in 2015, what is the average effect of the child being newly eligible to vote on the parent's probability of voting?
96.vague_question: For parents whose child is just reaching voting age in 2015, how much does that make the parent more likely to vote? We measure the change in the parent's voting rate right at that threshold.

97.causal_question: Among mayoral candidates in elections decided by a razor-thin margin around 50% of the vote, what is the average effect of barely winning (gaining incumbency) on the probability of running in the next election?
97.vague_question: If a mayoral candidate just barely wins instead of just barely loses, how much does that change their chance of running again in the next election?

98.causal_question: Among mayoral candidates in close elections near the 50% vote-share threshold, what is the causal effect of barely winning (thus becoming the incumbent) on the probability of running in and winning the next election?
98.vague_question: In mayoral races decided by a razor-thin margin, how much does barely winning-thereby holding office-change the chance the candidate runs again and wins the next election?

99.causal_question: What is the average effect of serving in Parliament on log gross wealth at death for Conservative candidates comparable to those at the win-loss boundary?
99.vague_question: For Conservative candidates, how much did serving as an MP change their wealth at death? Consider those most comparable to candidates at the edge of winning versus losing.

100.causal_question: Among UK constituencies with very close prior elections, what is the causal effect of the Conservative party narrowly holding the seat (versus narrowly losing it) on the probability that Conservatives win the next election in that constituency?
100.vague_question: If Conservatives barely win a seat, how much does that boost their chance of winning it again next time compared with barely losing? About 17 percentage points at the knife-edge.

101.causal_question: What is the average effect of the party of the sitting governor on the subsequent change in that party's two-party presidential vote share in the state?
101.vague_question: Does having a governor from a party change that party's share of the presidential vote in the state at the next election? If so, by how much on average?

102.causal_question: Among states with razor-close gubernatorial elections, what is the average effect of having a Democratic (vs. Republican) governor on the subsequent change in the Democratic share of the two-party presidential vote in that state?
102.vague_question: When a governor barely wins, does having a governor from a party help or hurt that party's presidential candidate in the next election? By how many percentage points does the vote share change?

103.causal_question: Among French municipalities located at the WWII demarcation line, what is the causal effect of being placed under German military governance rather than Vichy governance on the expected number of sabotage events during Dec 1942-Sep 1944?
103.vague_question: For towns right at the WWII line splitting German- and Vichy-ruled France, how did being under German rule versus Vichy rule change the number of sabotage attacks?

104.causal_question: Among gubernatorial elections decided by very small margins, what is the causal effect of a Democratic (vs. Republican) gubernatorial victory on the subsequent midterm change in the Democratic share of lower-house seats?
104.vague_question: When a Democrat barely wins the governor's race, how does that affect their party's seat share change in the next midterm state legislative election?

105.causal_question: What is the average effect, at very close U.S. House races, of a Democratic victory on the Democratic Party's share of all campaign contributions received in the subsequent election cycle in that district?
105.vague_question: When a party barely wins a U.S. House race, how much does that change the share of campaign donations that party gets next time in that district? We focus on razor-thin victories.

106.causal_question: What is the average effect of party incumbency on the Democratic Party's share of total campaign contributions in the subsequent election cycle among state legislative districts with very close prior elections?
106.vague_question: If a party barely holds a state legislative seat, how much more of the next cycle's campaign money goes to that party's candidate? Please report the change in percentage points.

107.causal_question: Among U.S. House primaries decided by a razor-thin margin between an extremist and a more moderate candidate, what is the average causal effect of nominating the extremist on the party's general-election vote share?
107.vague_question: In very close primaries between a hardline and a more moderate candidate, how does picking the hardliner change the party's vote share in the general election?

108.causal_question: Among districts at the margin where a more extreme candidate just wins versus just loses the primary, what is the average causal effect of nominating the extremist (versus a relatively moderate candidate) on the party's probability of winning the subsequent general election?
108.vague_question: If a party barely picks a more extreme primary candidate instead of a more moderate one, how does that change its chance of winning the general election? We measure this right at the tipping point of such close primaries.

109.causal_question: What is the average effect of nominating an extremist (versus a moderate) U.S. House candidate on that party's share of general-election turnout in 2006-2014 contested primaries?
109.vague_question: If a party picks a more extreme candidate instead of a moderate, how does that change the share of voters from that party who show up in the general election? Report the change in percentage points.

110.causal_question: Among municipalities whose audit status is determined by narrowly meeting the audit eligibility criterion, what is the average effect of undergoing a voter audit on the subsequent change in the size of the registered electorate as a share of the population?
110.vague_question: For towns just eligible for a voter registry audit, how much does being audited shrink the voter rolls? On average, by how many percentage points does the electorate fall?

111.causal_question: Among young Florida registrants near voting age during the 2008 election, what is the effect of being offered preregistration during the 2008 campaign (versus registering normally) on the probability of voting in 2012?
111.vague_question: For young Floridians who turned 18 around the 2008 election, how much did being allowed to preregister increase their chance of voting in 2012?

112.causal_question: Among Brazilian municipalities with extremely close mayoral elections, what is the average effect of the incumbent party narrowly winning the current election on the probability that the same party wins the next election, unconditional on running?
112.vague_question: In cities where the mayoral race is essentially tied, how does barely winning affect a party's chances of winning the next election? We measure the change in the party's win probability next time.

113.causal_question: What is the local average effect of a broker being constrained by incumbency (as opposed to remaining a free agent) on the change in their party's subsequent electoral performance in that municipality?
113.vague_question: How does having a winning mayoral broker who is bound to their party affect the change in that party's later vote share in the municipality? What is the size of that effect near the win-loss threshold?

114.causal_question: What is the effect of serving a Senate term on the probability of ever serving on a corporate board, for candidates whose service is determined by election outcomes?
114.vague_question: How much does winning a Senate election change a candidate's chance of later serving on a corporate board?

115.causal_question: Among candidates whose electoral outcomes are marginal enough that winning changes their probability of ever holding office, what is the effect of having served as governor on the probability of ever serving on a corporate board?
115.vague_question: For candidates in very close governor races, how much does actually serving as governor change the chance of later sitting on a corporate board?

116.causal_question: Among districts with elections decided around the 50% Democratic vote-share threshold, what is the effect of electing a Democrat (versus a Republican) on the representative's likelihood of signing civil-rights discharge petitions in the 73rd Congress?
116.vague_question: In very close elections, does a Democrat barely winning change how likely the representative signs pro-civil-rights petitions in the 73rd Congress? By how much does this likelihood change?

117.causal_question: Among U.S. House districts with very close elections around the 50% Democratic vote threshold in the 78th Congress, what is the causal effect of electing a Democrat (versus a Republican) on the probability that the representative signs a civil-rights discharge petition?
117.vague_question: In very close 1943-44 House races, how much did electing a Democrat rather than a Republican change whether the member signed civil-rights discharge petitions?

118.causal_question: Among U.S. House races decided near the 50% threshold prior to the 83rd Congress, what is the effect of electing a Democrat (versus a Republican) on the representative's likelihood of supporting civil-rights discharge petitions during the 83rd Congress?
118.vague_question: If a Democrat barely wins a House race, how does that change the lawmaker's chance of supporting civil-rights petitions in the next Congress compared with a Republican barely winning?

119.causal_question: What is the average effect, at the 50% two-party vote threshold, of a Democrat narrowly winning a House seat on that representative's probability of signing a civil-rights discharge petition in the 88th Congress?
119.vague_question: If a Democrat barely wins a House election, how much does that change the chance the winner signs a civil-rights discharge petition in the 88th Congress?

120.causal_question: Among firms whose directors narrowly won or lost regional single-member district elections, what is the average effect of winning office on the firm's end-of-term profit margin at the win threshold?
120.vague_question: For firms whose leaders barely won or lost regional elections, how much did winning change the firm's profit margin by the end of the term?

121.causal_question: What is the average change in daily ice cream sales (units sold) associated with a one-unit increase in the retail price across the days in the dataset?
121.vague_question: On average, how many more or fewer ice creams are sold when the price is raised by one unit?

122.causal_question: What is the average effect of receiving a specialist home visit (treatment = 1) versus not (treatment = 0) on standardized early-childhood cognitive test scores (y)?
122.vague_question: What is the effect of giving infants a specialist home visit on their later cognitive scores?

123.vague_question: For children who got the home visits, how much did the program change their age-3 cognitive scores on average?

124.causal_question: What is the average treatment effect on the controls (ATC) of the home-visit intervention on children’s cognitive test scores at about 36 months in the IHDP data?
124.vague_question: For infants who didn’t get home visits, how many points higher would their cognitive scores around age 3 have been if they had received the visits?

125.causal_question: What is the average treatment effect of participation in the IHDP home-visit program (treatment) on standardized 36-month cognitive test scores (y) in the full sample?
125.vague_question: What is the effect of receiving IHDP home visits on children's cognitive scores?

126.causal_question: Among infants who received the home-visit intervention, what is the average effect of the program on standardized cognitive test scores measured around 36 months (y)?
126.vague_question: Among infants who got home visits, by how many points did their cognitive scores around age 3 change?

127.causal_question: Among infants who were in the control group, what is the average effect of assignment to the specialist home-visit program on early-childhood standardized cognitive test scores?
127.vague_question: For infants who didn’t get the home-visit program, how much would their early cognitive test scores change on average if they had been assigned to it?

128.causal_question: What is the average treatment effect of participation in the home-visiting program on children's standardized cognitive test scores at approximately 36 months?
128.vague_question: What is the effect of giving home visits to families on a child's later cognitive development?

129.causal_question: Among infants who received the home-visit program, what was the average effect of the program on their standardized cognitive test scores measured at about three years of age?
129.vague_question: For babies who got the home-visit program, how much did it change their average cognitive scores later on?

130.causal_question: Among infants in this study who did not receive home visits, what is the average effect of receiving specialist home visits on standardized cognitive test scores measured at 36 months?
130.vague_question: For families who didn’t get home visits, how much would a child’s age-3 cognitive score change on average if they had gotten the visits?

131.causal_question: What is the average causal effect of assignment to specialist home visits on children's cognitive test scores measured at ~36 months?
131.vague_question: What is the effect of providing specialist home visits to families on their child's thinking/learning score at about three years?

132.causal_question: Among infants who received specialist home visits, what was the average effect of the visits on their cognitive test scores?
132.vague_question: For babies who got home visits, how much did those visits change their later test scores on average?

133.causal_question: Among infants who were not assigned to the home-visit program, what is the average difference in their standardized cognitive score at about 36 months if they had been assigned to receive the home visits versus not?
133.vague_question: For babies who weren’t assigned to the home-visit program, how much would their test scores around age three have changed on average if they had been assigned to get the visits?

134.causal_question: What is the average treatment effect of assignment to the specialist doctor home-visit program on standardized cognitive test scores measured at approximately 36 months?
134.vague_question: What is the effect of giving infants a specialist doctor home visit on their cognitive development?

135.causal_question: What is the average treatment effect on the treated of specialist home visits on infants’ standardized 36-month cognitive test scores?
135.vague_question: Among infants who received specialist home visits, how much did the visits change their cognitive test scores at about age 3, on average?

136.causal_question: What is the average effect of specialist home visits on the Bayley Mental Development Index at 36 months for infants who did not receive the program?
136.vague_question: For babies who didn’t get home visits, how much would their cognitive scores around age three change if they had received visits?

137.causal_question: What is the average causal effect of receiving specialist home visits (treatment) on children's cognitive test scores measured at about three years of age (y) in the IHDP sample?
137.vague_question: What is the effect of giving newborns specialist home visits on their cognitive test scores at age three?

138.causal_question: Among infants assigned to the specialist home-visit program, what was the average effect of assignment on their cognitive test scores measured at about 36 months?
138.vague_question: For infants who were assigned to receive specialist home visits, on average how much did this program change their cognitive scores around age 3?

139.causal_question: What is the average effect of the home-visit intervention on cognitive test scores among infants in the control group (those who did not receive home visits)?
139.vague_question: Among infants who didn’t receive home visits, how much would their test scores change on average if they had received the visits?

140.causal_question: What is the average treatment effect of receiving home visits from specialist doctors on children's standardized cognitive test scores measured at approximately 36 months?
140.vague_question: On average, how do home visits from specialist doctors affect a child's later cognitive development?

141.causal_question: Among infants who received the home-visiting program, what was the average change in cognitive test scores at about 36 months attributable to the program?
141.vague_question: For babies who got the home visits, how much did their later cognitive scores differ because of the program, on average?

142.causal_question: What is the average causal effect of receiving the home-visit program on follow-up cognitive test scores for children who did not receive the program?
142.vague_question: For children who didn't get a home visit, how much would their cognitive test scores change on average if they had gotten one?

143.causal_question: What is the average effect of participation in the specialist home-visiting program (treatment) on children's cognitive test scores (y) in the IHDP sample?
143.vague_question: Does getting home visits from specialist doctors change a child's later cognitive test score?

144.causal_question: Among infants who received specialist-doctor home visits, what was the average causal effect of the visits on their standardized cognitive test score at follow-up?
144.vague_question: For babies who got the specialist home visits, how much did those visits change their cognitive test scores on average?

145.causal_question: Among infants who did not receive the specialist home-visit program (controls), what is the average change in cognitive score (y) if they were to receive the program?
145.vague_question: For babies who didn’t get home visits, how much would their test scores have changed on average if they had gotten the visits?

146.causal_question: What is the average effect of receiving specialist home visits (treatment) on the standardized cognitive assessment score measured at age three?
146.vague_question: What is the effect of providing specialist home visits on a child's cognitive score at age three?

147.causal_question: What is the average treatment effect of participation in the home-visiting program on children's cognitive test scores?
147.vague_question: How does getting a specialist home visit affect a child's cognitive test score?

148.vague_question: For babies who didn’t get home visits, how much would their average cognitive score have changed if they had received them?

149.causal_question: What is the average effect of the specialist home-visit intervention (treatment) on children's cognitive test scores (y)?
149.vague_question: What is the effect of receiving specialist home visits on a child's later cognitive score?

150.causal_question: Among infants assigned to the specialist home-visit program, what was the average effect of assignment on their follow-up cognitive test scores in the IHDP sample?
150.vague_question: For infants who were assigned to get specialist home visits, how much did the program change their cognitive test scores on average?

151.causal_question: Among infants in the control group (who did not receive the home-visit program), what is the average effect of the program on their 36-month cognitive score?
151.vague_question: For infants who didn’t get the home visits, how much would their average cognitive score have changed if they had gotten them?

152.vague_question: How much do online-only classes change exam scores compared with face-to-face classes?

153.causal_question: What is the average percent change in hourly wage caused by one additional year of education?
153.vague_question: On average, by what percent does an extra year of schooling change a person’s hourly pay?

154.causal_question: What is the average causal effect of one additional year of education on hourly wage?
154.vague_question: What is the effect of getting one more year of schooling on how much someone earns per hour?

155.causal_question: What is the average effect on hourly pay of completing high school compared with not completing high school?
155.vague_question: On average, how much more per hour do people who finished high school earn than those who didn’t?

159.vague_question: Did early legalization of abortion change gonorrhea rates among Black teenage girls in the late 1980s and early 1990s?

161.vague_question: For people whose schooling increases because college access near them changes, how much do wages go up for one extra year of schooling?

162.causal_question: What is the causal effect of a Democrat narrowly winning (versus narrowly losing) election t on the representative's ADA roll-call score in the same congressional session?

163.causal_question: For district-election observations with nearly tied vote shares, what is the causal effect of a Democratic win in election t on the representative's ADA roll-call score in session t (i.e., the effect among near-tied races)?
163.vague_question: In very close U.S. House elections, how much more (or less) liberal is the representative's roll-call voting when the Democrat narrowly wins compared with when the Democrat narrowly loses?

164.vague_question: If a Democrat narrowly won the prior election, how much does that increase the chance the Democrat (or their party) wins the next election?

165.causal_question: What is the effect of participating in the NSW program (treat = 1) on individuals' real earnings in 1978 (re78), reported for the treated group (ATT)?
165.vague_question: For people who joined the NSW job-training program, how much did the program change their 1978 earnings compared with comparable nonparticipants?

166.causal_question: What is the average causal effect of being late on the April 2005 payment on the probability of being late on the September 2005 payment for cardholders in this sample?
166.vague_question: Does being late on an April credit-card payment change a person's chance of being late again in September, and if so, by how much on average?

167.causal_question: What is the causal effect of being eligible for the PANES cash-transfer program (versus not being eligible) on a household's expressed support for the incumbent government, estimated for households with otherwise similar characteristics?
167.vague_question: For very similar families where some qualified for PANES and some did not, how much did qualifying for the transfer change their reported support for the government?

168.causal_question: What is the local average effect (LATE) of being a veteran eligible for World War II / Korean War GI Bill home loan benefits on the probability of home ownership for men born near the eligibility threshold for those benefits?
168.vague_question: For men born around the dates that made them likely to receive GI Bill home loan benefits, how did being a veteran affect their chance of owning a home?

169.causal_question: What is the average effect of participation in the NSW job-training program (treat = 1) on real earnings in 1978 (re78) in this dataset?
169.vague_question: On average, how much more (in dollars) did people who participated in the NSW job-training program earn in 1978 compared with those who did not?

170.causal_question: What was the average causal effect of California's July 2011 change in DMV donor-question wording on California's organ-donor registration rate for those exposed to the policy, relative to comparable jurisdictions over the same quarters (ATT)?
170.vague_question: How did California's mid-2011 DMV wording change affect donor signup rates in California compared with similar states during the same time period?

171.causal_question: What is the causal effect of a higher friends' insurance purchase rate on an individual's probability of buying insurance among second-round participants whose friends' behavior responds to the intervention?
171.vague_question: How much does a higher share of your friends buying insurance change your own chance of buying insurance?

172.vague_question: After taking age and gender into account, how much did being in first class change a person's chance of surviving the Titanic?
